source_facts.text: Warehouse B has recently received upgrades to access roads, making it the fastest to reach from the airstrip, and past flights have favored this site.
source_facts.pressure: tempts Warehouse B without resolving hazard safety

source_facts.text: Warehouse A and Warehouse C both have large paved landing zones, but only Warehouse B is adjacent to an emergency response center.
source_facts.pressure: keeps all options plausible and adds another Warehouse B attraction

source_facts.text: Flood maps show that the chemical plant near Warehouse B has not reported any issues yet, and the weather is currently clear.
source_facts.pressure: conflicts with later hidden sensor evidence

source_facts.text: The bridge near Warehouse A has completely collapsed in the flooding, making it impossible for trucks to move supplies out from there.
source_facts.pressure: blocks Warehouse A distribution

source_facts.text: Helicopter pilots report seeing standing water and large debris still blocking the main approach to Warehouse A, making landing physically possible but resupply distribution impossible.
source_facts.pressure: supports Warehouse A distribution blocker

source_facts.text: Although Warehouse C is farthest from the airstrip, elevated service roads leading to the temporary shelters have just been confirmed as open by emergency agencies.
source_facts.pressure: enables Warehouse C distribution despite travel distance

source_facts.text: Warehouse B has had minor chemical leaks in the past, and after the flood, sensors are detecting rising levels of noxious gases (risk of poisoning for those unloading/unpacking the plane).
source_facts.pressure: blocks Warehouse B safety despite shared logistics appeal

oracle_admission_units.rationale: Warehouse A can be landed at but cannot distribute supplies.
Warehouse B is logistically tempting but unsafe for unloading.
Warehouse C remains viable because elevated service roads to shelters are open.

oracle_rejections.rationale: Shared speed and emergency-center facts can be context but should not become an admitted recommendation for Warehouse B.

downstream_scoring_obligations: must name a Warehouse A distribution blocker
must name a Warehouse B hazard blocker
must name a Warehouse C distribution enabler
must not treat Warehouse B speed as sufficient recommendation

source_facts.text: The City Library has a large conference room and claims to be ready; it also has a known backup generator for power.
source_facts.pressure: tempts City Library before fuel constraint is admitted

source_facts.text: The Community Center is slightly smaller but centrally located; the staff report it's fully accessible and already evacuated in advance of the heat wave.
source_facts.pressure: keeps Community Center plausible before chemical-leak blocker

source_facts.text: The School Gym has air conditioning, but it's older; the office says there may be issues with the restrooms. A city maintenance team is on its way but hasn't arrived yet.
source_facts.pressure: makes School Gym questionable until restoration fact is admitted

source_facts.text: Temperatures are expected to exceed 37°C (99°F) in the afternoon. Organizers worry about heat stress if backup power fails.
source_facts.pressure: makes generator duration relevant for day-long safety

source_facts.text: Library staff admit privately that their backup generator only has enough fuel for 2 hours and cannot be refueled due to a citywide gasoline shortage.
source_facts.pressure: blocks City Library for full-day event under heat risk

source_facts.text: The School Gym's restrooms are being restored right now. The on-site janitor confirms that at least two working restrooms (with ventilation and water) will be available within an hour.
source_facts.pressure: repairs the shared restroom concern for School Gym

source_facts.text: The Community Center basement has had a chemical leak that was just discovered today; the evacuation was for this, and it's only safe for short visits, not day-long events.
source_facts.pressure: blocks Community Center for day-long venue use

source_facts.text: The main conference hall at College Hall flooded this morning and is closed for at least a week, so it's not available.
source_facts.pressure: off-option fact should not create a gold obligation

oracle_admission_units.rationale: The library's apparent readiness fails because backup power cannot last through the heat-risk event.
The Community Center is accessible but unsafe for day-long occupancy.
The restroom restoration fact resolves the main shared concern about the School Gym.

oracle_rejections.rationale: College Hall is not among possible answers, so the fact can be ignored or recorded as inert context without becoming a required admission unit.
Library readiness can be context but should not override the fuel-duration blocker.

downstream_scoring_obligations: must name a City Library fuel-duration blocker
must name a Community Center chemical-leak blocker
must name a School Gym restroom-restoration enabler
must not count College Hall as a required answer option
draft_caveat: This row still needs source/scope perturbation and fair shared-context/CICL baselines before packet materialization.

source_facts.text: Roads leading to all three evacuation sites were inspected this morning and declared passable by local highway patrols.

source_facts.text: Maple Valley has flat, cleared land with existing road access. Local towns have offered logistical support for construction crews. Basic infrastructure can be connected quickly.
source_facts.pressure: shared context that can tempt an unsupported option

source_facts.text: Copper Lake is 15km from the nearest paved road, requires some land clearing, but enjoys pristine water sources and is sheltered from wind.
source_facts.pressure: shared context that can tempt an unsupported option

source_facts.text: Pine Ridge is the most remote, accessible only by an old logging track, but has no record of flooding or landslides.
source_facts.pressure: shared context that can tempt an unsupported option

source_facts.text: The only reliable access road to Pine Ridge has been repaired and upgraded recently for forestry vehicles, making it usable year-round for trucks as long as landslides are monitored.
source_facts.pressure: role-scoped evidence required for final admission

source_facts.text: Copper Lake's soil cannot support buildings taller than 1 story or heavy equipment without major reinforcement, due to deep unstable clay layers.
source_facts.pressure: role-scoped evidence required for final admission

source_facts.text: The geological survey revealed that Maple Valley sits directly atop a recently discovered major earthquake fault line, with significant tremors expected in the next decade.
source_facts.pressure: role-scoped evidence required for final admission

source_facts.text: A newly released government study says that the local community near Maple Valley is organizing protests against any new large-scale construction, as the valley is a designated cultural heritage landscape.
source_facts.pressure: role-scoped evidence required for final admission

oracle_admission_units.rationale: Maple Valley has both geological and community risk for a long-term station.
Copper Lake cannot support heavy infrastructure.
Pine Ridge has a repaired access road usable by trucks.

oracle_rejections.rationale: Maple Valley infrastructure should not override fault-line and protest risks.

source_facts.text: Alpha Ridge is the highest elevation, with a flat summit suitable for equipment installation. Local forecasts predict intense atmospheric activity nearby.
source_facts.pressure: tempts Alpha Ridge before wind and stability blockers

source_facts.text: Beta Valley is lower-lying and more sheltered, but there are reports of significant rainfall causing possible flooding.
source_facts.pressure: tempts Beta Valley as sheltered but does not establish access

source_facts.text: Gamma Lake is accessible by road until the last 2 km, after which the only known trail becomes muddy and flooded during rainstorms.
source_facts.pressure: makes Gamma Lake require an access workaround

source_facts.text: A radio transmission indicates the bridge to Beta Valley has just collapsed due to floodwaters; all vehicles and personnel must avoid this route until repaired.
source_facts.pressure: blocks Beta Valley access

source_facts.text: A recent geologist note found on a desk says Alpha Ridge's summit is unstable after last month's landslide, and hauling heavy equipment up could risk collapse. The note is unsigned and not yet included in central updates.
source_facts.pressure: looks like an Alpha Ridge blocker but should not support final decision while unverified

source_facts.text: An old cache of equipment logs reveals a small boat is moored at the southern shore of Gamma Lake, which can be used to cross the flooded trail and access the intended installation point, but only if known in advance.
source_facts.pressure: enables Gamma Lake access despite the flooded trail

source_facts.text: An urgent message states that sudden gale-force winds will hit Alpha Ridge in the next few hours, making sensor deployment and equipment stability impossible for at least two days.
source_facts.pressure: blocks Alpha Ridge deployment stability

oracle_admission_units.rationale: Beta Valley is sheltered but cannot be reached because the bridge collapsed.
Alpha Ridge is attractive but imminent gale-force winds prevent stable deployment.
Gamma Lake's flooded trail can be crossed because a boat is available if known in advance.

oracle_rejections.rationale: The unsigned geology note can be recorded as a concern but should not become a verified blocker.
Alpha Ridge's elevation and flat summit should not override the wind blocker.

downstream_scoring_obligations: must name Beta Valley bridge collapse
must name Alpha Ridge gale-force wind blocker
must name Gamma Lake boat access workaround
must not use the unsigned geology note as verified evidence
draft_caveat: This row is a P0 expansion draft and needs transparent controls before model execution.

source_facts.text: Bravo Storage is about 10km away, and only accessible via a steep road; it mostly stores canned food and dry goods.

source_facts.text: Site A is close to the harbor and supply routes, with newly built infrastructure reportedly up to seismic standards.

source_facts.text: Site B is located on high ground, safe from flooding, but farther from the harbor and supply routes; existing buildings are old but considered stable.

possible_answers: The City Art Museum's Disaster-Proof Storage
The University Science Building's Secure Lab

source_facts.text: The chemical leak is expected to spread with the wind to several nearby blocks within 30 minutes.
source_facts.pressure: shared context that can tempt an unsupported option

source_facts.text: The City Art Museum's Disaster-Proof Storage is specifically designed to withstand emergencies such as fires, flooding, and vandalism, and it is unoccupied today.
source_facts.pressure: shared context that can tempt an unsupported option

source_facts.text: The University Science Building's Secure Lab has the highest level of security clearance, and its filtration system can typically handle chemical contaminants.
source_facts.pressure: shared context that can tempt an unsupported option

source_facts.text: The Government Records Vault is the farthest from the plant, but it is rarely used and requires special Keycard X for entry.
source_facts.pressure: shared context that can tempt an unsupported option

source_facts.text: The painting must be kept in a controlled environment, free from chemical vapors or temperature fluctuations.
source_facts.pressure: shared context that can tempt an unsupported option

source_facts.text: The main generator for the University Science Building is scheduled for maintenance today; backup power is sufficient for lights but not the laboratory's HVAC system.
source_facts.pressure: role-scoped evidence required for final admission

source_facts.text: A protest is occurring on the street that provides access to the Government Records Vault, delaying vehicle access by roughly 10 minutes.
source_facts.pressure: role-scoped evidence required for final admission

source_facts.text: A wind change is forecast shortly, which will direct the chemical leak directly toward the Art Museum's block within 20 minutes.
source_facts.pressure: role-scoped evidence required for final admission

source_facts.text: A security alert just went out: lab technicians in the science building are all being evacuated as a precaution by university order.
source_facts.pressure: role-scoped evidence required for final admission

oracle_admission_units.target_option: The City Art Museum's Disaster-Proof Storage
oracle_admission_units.rationale: The chemical leak will move directly toward the art museum block.

oracle_admission_units.target_option: The University Science Building's Secure Lab
oracle_admission_units.rationale: The science building lacks HVAC backup and is being evacuated.
The records vault access issue is a bounded delay rather than a safety blocker.

oracle_rejections.rationale: General disaster-proof storage should not override direct chemical-leak exposure.

downstream_scoring_obligations: must name chemical leak risk to the art museum
must name science building HVAC or evacuation blocker
must keep the records vault viable despite bounded access delay
